title: DoorDash Surpasses Q1 Earnings Estimates
text: DoorDash reported Q1 earnings that surpassed consensus analyst estimates, demonstrating strong profitability execution despite revenue headwinds. The company posted GAAP earnings per share of $0.42, beating analyst consensus estimates of $0.37 by 13.5%. This significant earnings beat reflects the company's operational efficiency improvements and higher-margin revenue contributions from advertising and subscriptions. Revenue of $4.04 billion fell short of $4.14 billion estimates, but the earnings surprise dominated investor focus. Adjusted EBITDA of $754 million exceeded analyst estimates of $741.5 million, showing robust cash generation. The company's marketplace GOV of $31.6 billion exceeded analyst expectations of $31.5 billion, demonstrating strong underlying demand metrics. Gross margin came in at 51.9%, ahead of analyst estimates of 51.6%. Strong profitability in the face of revenue pressure reflects the company's strategic shift toward higher-margin revenue streams and improved operational leverage. The stock reacted positively to the results, with investors rewarding operational efficiency and forward guidance. The company's confident outlook for Q2, projecting marketplace GOV of $32.4-$33.4 billion well above consensus expectations, signaled management confidence in sustained demand momentum.
